content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (Info.plist lines 6-9), with no location usage found anywhere in the codebase (only camera use in App/ScannerView.swift).
Pierre: Apple rejects purpose strings that are placeholders or don't explain the specific benefit to the user — "TODO" will trigger an immediate rejection, and "This app needs camera access" is too generic since it doesn't mention document scanning. Also, since no code actually requests location, this unused permission should either be removed from Info.plist or implemented and documented properly. Rewrite the camera string to something like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and remove the location key if it's unused.
